RADIO, TELEVISION AND CINEMA STUDIO

RTC Studio is a production space equipped with rich technical infrastructure, where students of the Radio, Television and Cinema Department receive hands-on training. Located within the Media Lab, the studio is available to students for both coursework and individual projects. Starting from their first year, students have the opportunity to gain practical experience in key production processes such as camera operation, lighting techniques, sound design, green screen applications, and scriptwriting. The equipment in the studio is regularly updated to meet current industry standards. All equipment in the RTS Studio is available for students to use free of charge through a reservation system. In addition, the studio includes an editing room equipped with iMac computers running Adobe software, allowing students to carry out editing and post-production work. This space is accessible 24/7 for both class assignments and personal projects. The studio also houses a soundproof podcast recording room, offering a professional environment for students to produce radio programs, podcast projects, and sound design work. RTS Studio not only provides technical resources, but also serves as a creative space that supports production, encourages critical thinking, and helps students gain valuable industry experience.
